title: Continuous Eval in Production
concept: Running evaluation suites automatically against random production logs to monitor drift over time.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of shadow testing an AI agent update?

options.text: To compare the performance of the new version to the current one.
To increase the amount of data collected.
To permanently replace the old version immediately.

exercises.explain: The purpose of shadow testing is to evaluate how a new AI agent version performs alongside the current one using live data.

cards.title: Let's Monitor Drift!
cards.body: Just like a gardener checks the health of plants over time, we check AI output to see if it's still accurate and reliable.

cards.title: What is Continuous Evaluation?
cards.body: Continuous Evaluation involves regularly testing the performance of an AI system against new data to identify any performance issues.

cards.title: Like a Plant in Different Seasons
cards.body: If a plant grows well in summer but not in winter, we need to regularly check its health. Similarly, AI systems can change over time, so we monitor them continuously.

cards.title: Example of Continuous Eval
cards.body: Imagine an AI model for spam detection. If it starts to misclassify emails, continuous evaluation helps us catch this drift quickly.

cards.title: Myth: One-time Testing is Enough
cards.body: Some think they can test once and forget about it. However, just like weather changes, the performance of AI models can drift.

cards.title: Monitoring Production Logs
cards.body: We can run evaluation tests on real logs to see how our AI performs.

right.text: Regular checks reveal changes in accuracy.

exercises.prompt: What is the main advantage of continuous evaluation?

options.text: It replaces all old AI systems with new ones.
It helps identify performance drift over time.
It reduces the amount of data needed for testing.
It increases the number of updates required.

exercises.explain: The main advantage of continuous evaluation is that it helps detect performance drift over time, allowing teams to maintain accuracy.

exercises.prompt: Continuous evaluation is only necessary once a year.
exercises.explain: False. Continuous evaluation should happen regularly to catch any drift in performance as it occurs.

exercises.prompt: Continuous evaluation is important because it helps identify ___ over time.
exercises.sentence: Continuous evaluation is important because it helps identify drift over time.
exercises.explain: Drift is the correct answer as it refers to changes in performance that occur over time.

exercises.prompt: Which statement describes continuous evaluation best?

options.text: Testing occurs once after an update.
Monitoring performance regularly against fresh data.
It only happens during scheduled maintenance.

exercises.explain: Monitoring performance regularly against fresh data best describes continuous evaluation, as it ensures ongoing accuracy.

exercises.prompt: Why is it crucial to run evaluation suites automatically against production logs?

options.text: To find and fix drift quickly before it affects users.
To stop all AI updates permanently.

exercises.explain: Running evaluation suites against production logs is crucial to quickly identify and address drift, ensuring that users receive accurate and reliable AI outputs.
